description: §41.16 — supplying BOTH `pick=[...]` AND `omit=[...]` on the same `<tableFor>` fires E-TABLEFOR-PICK-OMIT-CONFLICT. Both name VALID fields (`pick=["name"]`, `omit=["id"]`), so this pins the mutual-exclusivity rule specifically, NOT a field-validity error — the two transforms are inverses (pick names the only fields to render; omit names fields to exclude) and cannot be combined. Mirror of E-FORMFOR-PICK-OMIT-CONFLICT (§41.14) + E-SCHEMAFOR-PICK-OMIT-CONFLICT (§41.15). Because the fields are valid, E-TABLEFOR-PICK-INVALID-FIELD / E-TABLEFOR-OMIT-INVALID-FIELD MUST NOT fire. (Incidental W-PROGRAM-001 + W-STDLIB-SEED-FAILCLOSED co-fire; superset match.)
rationale: §41.16 E-TABLEFOR-PICK-OMIT-CONFLICT: `pick:` and `omit:` are mutually exclusive; supplying both on one call is rejected. Fix: use exactly one per call, or layer Pick over Omit at the type level and pass the derived type.
